title: Vincetoxicum miquelianum
taxonomy: Vincetoxicum miquelianum belongs to the family Apocynaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander. This plant is native to East Asia and is a perennial species. It is classified within the genus Vincetoxicum, which is a group of highly toxic plants. The genus Vincetoxicum is further classified within the subfamily Asclepiadoideae, which is a subfamily of plants within the Apocynaceae family. Vincetoxicum miquelianum's classification helps in understanding its relationships with other plants and its unique characteristics.

intro: Vincetoxicum miquelianum, also known as Miquel's swallowwort, is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, commonly used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant has been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anticancer properties, making it a valuable resource for the treatment of various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Vincetoxicum miquelianum include vincetoxin, a cardenolide with potential therapeutic applications, as well as other glycosides and alkaloids that contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Vincetoxicum miquelianum is commonly prepared as a decoction or tincture, often in combination with other herbs, to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive disorders.
